notes: REGRESSION: Chr3:16,439,674 lost. Phase 1 still VALIDATEs it high/element=yes, but Phase 1.5 transgene-positive BLAST does not retain its clip sequences, so the site is not assembled. The 4 sites that survive Phase 1.5 (Chr3_29074002, Chr3_31443563, Chr2_27762894, Chr8_18596286) are all FP/UNK in Phase 4. Likely cause: pre-existing edge case where the real T-DNA clips are identified as "univec" (the 5p clip mapped to gnl|uv|KM062046.1-type seqs in Phase 1) and the element_db-required transgene-positive policy filters them out. Feature branch does not cause this regression; common_payload.fa (9 bacterial/viral seqs) adds 2 clip hits, too few to rescue Chr3:16,439,674.

A2_3 step 5 was killed after ~60 min (assembly phase was processing 3-4 of 27 transgene-positive sites and would have needed several more hours given 27 sites + per-site assembly rounds). Key positive signal: Phase 1 VALIDATED SLM_r2.0ch01:91,002,744 as high/element=yes with 5p=univec (76bp) and 3p=NODE_1_length_4160_cov_7.260103 from s04b contigs at 100%/124bp — confirming --extra-element-db successfully recruits the sample-specific T-DNA backbone. ch01_91002744 was 3rd of 27 sites queued for assembly when run was killed, so no Phase 4 verdict was written. Worktree had to symlink SLM_r2.0.pmol.* from main db/ (worktree's db/ is a real directory, not a symlink, and was missing tomato refs).
